message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-crypto: cap COSE token + CWT claims CBOR decode at 64 KiB

Both CoseToken::from_cbor and CwtClaims::from_cbor previously called
ciborium::from_reader / CoseSign1::from_slice on unbounded input. FCP
capability tokens are ~300–500 bytes in practice, but these paths are
reachable from:

- fcp-host token verification on every authenticated request
- Mesh-forwarded tokens via signed control-plane messages
- Any future caller that plumbs untrusted bytes straight in

A crafted oversized CBOR payload (large bytestring claim, deeply-nested
value, etc.) would consume memory up to ciborium's internal limits and
spend CPU on deterministic-canonicalization / signature-prep work that
will ultimately be rejected. Fail-closed at the entry point instead.

- MAX_COSE_TOKEN_BYTES = 64 * 1024 (two orders of magnitude above any
  realistic capability token).
- Both entry points short-circuit with CryptoError::SerializationError
  before touching ciborium/coset.
- Three tests: rejects MAX+1 on each entry point, and confirms the cap
  is inclusive (a blob of exactly MAX_COSE_TOKEN_BYTES bytes passes the
  size check and fails on parse instead, proving the reject path is
  off-by-one safe).

Verified locally: cargo test -p fcp-crypto --lib rejects_oversized_payload
→ 2 passed, accepts_payload_at_cap → 1 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
